text:
Admission Date:  2004-09-20              Discharge Date:   1999-09-03

Date of Birth:  1971-12-13             Sex:   F

Service: SURGERY

Allergies:
Penicillins

Attending:Michelle
Chief Complaint:
abdominal pain, retching, nausea, vomiting

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
none


History of Present Illness:
Pt is a 73yo F who was tranferred from Select Specialty Hospital - Cleveland Gateway Hospital after
having presented with crampy epigastric pain radiating to the
back.  She had eaten a Michael meal at a party on Saturday, and
developed the aforementioned pain.  SEveral hours later, the
patient had retching, vomiting, and nausea.
She went to Select Specialty Hospital - Cleveland Gateway Hospital that evening, and reported an
"inflamed gallbladder," as well as a new L BBB on admission.
The patient was evaluated and treated at the hospital and
transferred to Lake City Community Hospital on 2004-09-20.
Currently, she denies any previous abdominal pain or similar
episodes or back pain.  She also denies any heart conditions,
chest pain, or shortness of breath.

The patient was sent with CT findings consistent with claculous
cholecystitis, as well as rising troponins to 0.4 and a new
LBBB.

Past Medical History:
osteoporosis, hypercholesterolemia, reportedly normal stress
test in 1999 or 1980?
hysterectomy

Social History:
Pt denies drug use or cigarettes.

Family History:
noncontributory

Physical Exam:
96.4    86     130/71     17     98% (2L NC)
NAD
EOMI
RRR NL s1s2
CTA b/l
soft abd, ND, + RUQ ttp
trace pedal edema
AAOx3
CN II-XII intact


Pertinent Results:
2004-09-20 10:52PM   WBC-16.9* RBC-3.54* HGB-10.9* HCT-31.7*
MCV-90 MCH-30.9 MCHC-34.5 RDW-13.3
2004-09-20 10:52PM   CK-MB-NotDone cTropnT-0.02*
2004-09-20 10:52PM   ALT(SGPT)-42* AST(SGOT)-31 LD(LDH)-190
CK(CPK)-60 ALK PHOS-106 AMYLASE-25 TOT BILI-0.6

Brief Hospital Course:
THe patient was admitted to the SICU on admission on 2004-09-20.
She was made NPO, put on IVF, and also put on vanco, gent and
flagyl.  Routine labs as well as cardiac enzymes were monitored;
a chest x-ray as well as an EKG was performed.  A cardiology
consult was called for evaluation of the new LBBB, previously
rising troponins, and an echo (from OSH) revealing a LVEF of 45%
with apical hypokinesis.

The patient was started on a beta blocker (metoprolol), aspirin,
and continued on her home medications.  On 1997-04-30, the patient
went for cardiac catheterization, which was normal--no CAD.

The patient's stay was complicated by several episodes of
diarrhea; she was c.diff negative, and placed on lactobacillus
ans psyllium wafers.

The patient's diet was advanced as appropriate and as tolerated
during her stay, and on 1999-09-03, the patient was discharged
home in good condition.

Medications on Admission:
Boniva, Tricor, Ca, MVI, baby asa

Discharge Medications:
1. Acetaminophen 325 mg Tablet Sig: 1-2 Tablets PO Q6H (every 6
hours) as needed for pain/headache/fever.
2. Metoprolol Tartrate 25 mg Tablet Sig: 0.5 Tablet PO BID (2
times a day) for 5 weeks.
Disp:*35 Tablet(s)* Refills:*1*
3. Psyllium 1.7 g Wafer Sig: One (1) Wafer PO DAILY (Daily).
4. Lactobacillus Acidophilus 500 million cell Tablet Sig: One
(1) Tablet PO twice a day as needed for indigestion: For
diarrhea.
5. Please follow up with your PCP regarding [**Name Initial (PRE) **] prescription for a
"statin". Thank you.
6. Fenofibrate Micronized 145 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO
Qdaily ().
7. Aspirin 325 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY (Daily).
8. Famotidine 20 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO Q12H (every 12
hours).
9. Ciprofloxacin 500 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO every
twelve (12) hours for 2 weeks.
Disp:*28 Tablet(s)* Refills:*0*
10. Metronidazole 500 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO TID (3
times a day) for 2 weeks.
Disp:*42 Tablet(s)* Refills:*0*
11. Vancomycin 250 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO Q6H (every
6 hours) for 10 days: 21 substitute with liquid if desired.
Thank you.
Disp:*40 Capsule(s)* Refills:*0*


Discharge Disposition:
Home

Discharge Diagnosis:
Cholecystitis/choledocholithiasis with EKG changes and elevated
cardiac enzymes.


Discharge Condition:
good


Discharge Instructions:
Please Danielle Morales MD or go to ER if you experience Temp>101.5, chest
pain, shortness of breath, severe nausea/vomiting, or severe
abdominal pain.

You have been started on a low residue diet, please follow the
directions given to you by nutrition.

Continue taking your antibiotics as written. Do not stop taking
unless told directly by Dr. Lang or his office.


Followup Instructions:
Please follow up with Dr. Lang; please call his office at
612-444-6404 to schedule a follow up appointment.
